<!--
Adapt — not every section applies. A bug fix might need only What / Why / Acceptance criteria.
A bigger feature wants the full set. Add a priority label (P1/P2/P3, default P2) and at least one
thematic label (UI/UX, gameplay, accessibility, hygiene, SEO, analytics, bug).
-->

## What
<!-- One or two sentences. Plain English. -->

## Why
<!-- Problem solved or player value. For bigger items, user-story framing works: "As a player, I want X so that Y." -->

## Must have
-

## Nice to have
-

## Details
<!-- Approach, constraints, options. Sub-headings OK for big items. Delete if not needed. -->

## Open questions
<!-- Only if decisions are pending. Don't invent them. Delete if none. -->
-

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ]

## Definition of done
<!-- The execution convention from CLAUDE.md. Every roadmap item runs at least this minimal cycle. -->
- [ ] **Discuss** — scope confirmed, assumptions/gray areas surfaced
- [ ] **Plan** — steps broken out (proportional to size)
- [ ] **Execute** — built with atomic commits
- [ ] **Review** — DA review → self-review passed before PR
- [ ] **QA** — Playwright e2e run for UI changes; for non-UI, QA proposed + confirmed (or consciously skipped)
